title: Bruins trade Fabian Lysell to Colorado for forward Ivan Ivan
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins traded forward Fabian Lysell to the Colorado Avalanche for forward Ivan Ivan.
Fabian Lysell, the 21st overall pick in the 2021 NHL Draft, played only 12 games with the Bruins.
Ivan Ivan scored 26 points in 66 AHL games last season and has NHL experience with the Avalanche.

title: Forget the celebrities in the crowd, France are the entertainers of the 2026 World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France defeated Norway 4-1 in the World Cup at Boston Stadium, showcasing a powerful and entertaining offense.
Kylian Mbappe and Ousmane Dembélé led France's attack, contributing assists and goals to their commanding performance.
Despite making lineup changes, both France's and Norway's players demonstrated high-level play, with France emerging as favorites.

title: Cape DA investigating alleged threats at Sandwich Middle High
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sandwich Middle High School officials and police investigate student threats identified by a safety app.
A student allegedly made threats against classmates, prompting police collaboration with the school's resource officer.
The involved student is barred from attending school for the rest of the year as investigations continue.

title: Man convicted after breaking into the wrong Boston apartment and murdering resident
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Victor Arrington, 40, convicted of first-degree murder in 2015 Dorchester home invasion, faces life sentence without parole.
Prosecutors used location data from Arrington's iPhone to secure conviction; first trial ended in mistrial.
Accomplices mistakenly targeted victim; Richard Long killed, fiancée survived, third accomplice killed in unrelated crime.

title: Some 20 New Englanders are on ‘American Ninja Warrior’ this season. Here’s your in-depth preview.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Season 18 of 'American Ninja Warrior' features 18 New Englanders, including 12 from Massachusetts, competing in the Eastern qualifiers.
Taylor Johnson and other ninjas train at Vitality Obstacle Fitness in Fall River, under coach Jordan Thurston.
Massachusetts has over 10 ninja gyms, contributing to the region's success in 'American Ninja Warrior' competitions.

title: Scotland came to Boston. Did romance follow?
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Scottish soccer fans visited Boston for the World Cup, filling the city with kilts and cheerful gatherings.
The Haven, a Scottish-themed restaurant in Boston, became a popular spot for fans despite Scotland's loss.
Locals were curious about potential romances with Scottish visitors, amid stories of loyalty and companionship.

title: 4 of the weirdest, most absurd soccer movies streaming now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Shaolin Soccer' blends soccer with Shaolin kung fu, becoming a cult classic since its 2001 Hong Kong release.
'Diamantino' is a 2018 political satire about a Portuguese soccer superstar, available on Kanopy and Hoopla.
'United Passions' is a notorious flop with a $30M budget, earning $918 in its opening weekend, available on Tubi.

title: Is the Trump administration on the verge of further endangering North Atlantic right whales?
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: The Trump administration is considering rolling back maritime speed regulations designed to protect endangered North Atlantic right whales.
Currently, speed limits for vessels 65 feet or longer are enforced in designated areas along the East Coast to reduce whale strikes.
Conservationists oppose deregulation, fearing it will threaten the right whale population, which includes approximately 380 individuals as of 2024.

title: Payton Tolle gets standing ovation after tossing seven scoreless innings vs. Yankees
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Payton Tolle pitched seven scoreless innings, allowing one hit and striking out seven, leading Red Sox to a 6-1 win over Yankees.
Tolle carried a perfect game into the sixth inning despite battling an illness before the game at Fenway Park.
Tolle has a 2.78 ERA this season and has dominated against the Yankees with 18 strikeouts across two starts.

title: 5 ways Celtics could improve lineup without trading Jaylen Brown
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Celtics consider trading Sam Hauser to gain salary cap flexibility and potentially acquire a big-name player.
Boston aims to acquire Trey Murphy by trading Derrick White and Baylor Scheierman without giving up Jaylen Brown.
Celtics explore adding center depth and discuss potential free agents like Mitchell Robinson or trading for Evan Mobley.
